sources.name: TASVideos Game Resources — NES / DuckTales
sources.notes: Speedrun technique documentation. Confirms Y sub-pixel formula '[current value + 51] mod 256' suggesting 256 sub-pixel system. Walk and pogo physics documented qualitatively.

notes: DuckTales is a Capcom NES game — physics share design DNA with Mega Man series (same developer, same era).
DEFINING MECHANIC: Scrooge McDuck's cane POGO — bouncing on the cane tip provides high jump and damages enemies/breaks blocks.
Sub-pixel system: TASVideos confirms 256 sub-pixel precision (formula '[current value + 51] mod 256').
Walk speed not explicitly documented — estimated from Capcom NES standards.
Pogo height is notably higher than standard jump — the game's traversal is built around the pogo.
Non-linear level select: 5 stages accessible in any order (Moon, Transylvania, Amazon, Himalayas, African Mines), similar to Mega Man.

subpixel_system.description: 256 sub-pixel precision confirmed by TASVideos Y sub-pixel formula '[current value + 51] mod 256'.

walk_speed.notes: Approximate based on Capcom NES standard (Mega Man series: 1.5 px/f). Not explicitly documented in reviewed sources. VERIFY against ROM.

ground_movement.notes: Appears to be instant velocity — consistent with Capcom NES titles of the era.

notes: Standard jump (pressing A without cane pogo) is shorter than the pogo jump.
Approximate from gameplay observation. VERIFY.
Players rarely use the standard jump for traversal — the pogo is almost always superior for height.

pogo._note: THE DEFINING MECHANIC. Scrooge's cane pogo transforms him from a slow old man into a high-speed platformer protagonist.
pogo.activation: Press Down + B while in the air, OR pressing B while on ground initiates pogo
notes: Pogo height is approximately double the standard jump height.
Pogo can be performed consecutively — bouncing rapidly allows crossing platforms without touching ground.
Each pogo bounce maintains or adds to the next bounce height if timed correctly.
Pogo on an enemy: kills/stuns the enemy and bounces Scrooge upward (same height as environmental pogo).
Pogo on breakable blocks: breaks the block and bounces Scrooge upward.
Pogo speed: the horizontal momentum during pogo is maintained from ground speed at pogo initiation.

chaining.description: Consecutive pogo bounces can be chained for rapid vertical+horizontal movement.
chaining.notes: Chain pogoing is the primary speed technique in DuckTales speedruns.

gravity.notes: Gravity is not explicitly documented in reviewed sources. VERIFY against Data Crystal RAM map.
gravity.qualitative: DuckTales gravity feels 'Capcom-standard' — similar arc weight to Mega Man. The pogo bounce overrides gravity briefly each contact.

cane_attack.description: Scrooge can swing the cane horizontally to hit enemies at head level.
notes: Cane swing is the horizontal attack (B while standing, no down input).
Cane swing has short reach but is instantaneous — no animation delay like CV whip.
Can hit chests and blocks horizontally.

level_structure.order: Player selects any stage in any order (like Mega Man)
notes: Each stage has a hidden treasure chest containing a valuable item.
Final boss available after all 5 stages are cleared.
Stage difficulty is relatively uniform — no obvious intended order (also like Mega Man).

scrooge_standing.notes: Scrooge is shorter than most NES protagonists (elderly, stooped posture). Hitbox narrower than sprite. Exact values need ROM verification.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: The pogo mechanic recontextualizes everything in the level. Surfaces aren't 'floors to land on' — they're 'trampolines to bounce off.' Enemy placement in DuckTales is fundamentally different from Mario because enemies are also pogo surfaces.
The non-linear stage select mirrors Mega Man's design but with a different feel — Scrooge's wealth-accumulation theme suggests you're exploring for treasure, not defeating bosses in a resistance arc.
The cane has two uses (pogo for traversal/damage, swing for horizontal hits) that serve different situations. This dual-use tool creates a light systems-thinking layer: 'which mode is better here?'

Scrooge's short stature and elderly character design in contrast with his extreme athletic pogo capability creates a charming disconnect. The game's aesthetics lean into this — he's a billionaire bouncing on his cane through supernatural environments.
